message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-xd6xi (chordal_graph_cliques MultiGraph)

Fixed in commit 0f5231d. Pre-existing MultiGraph failures
TestChordalGraphCliquesParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback
resolved as a side-effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-wu9dv","title":"parity: _FilteredDegreeView class name and repr diverge from nx's DegreeView","description":"fnx.bipartite.degrees(G, top) returns a tuple of (_FilteredDegreeView, _FilteredDegreeView). nx returns (DegreeView({1: 2, 3: 2}), DegreeView({0: 1, 2: 2, 4: 1})). Two issues: (1) class name '_FilteredDegreeView' differs from nx's 'DegreeView'. (2) repr shows just node-keys list ('_FilteredDegreeView([1, 3])') instead of nx's full dict repr ('DegreeView({1: 2, 3: 2})'). Drop-in code that prints these or compares reprs breaks. Fix: rename class to 'DegreeView' (or set __qualname__) and update __repr__ to match nx's dict format.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:14:35.680457671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:17:16.042991811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:17:16.041024306Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit d524522. _FilteredDegreeView now has class name 'DegreeView' and repr matching nx's DegreeView({...}) format. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-wvh8r","title":"parity: rescale_layout default scale=1.0 should be scale=1 to match networkx","description":"fnx.rescale_layout has default scale=1.0 (float) but networkx.drawing.layout.rescale_layout has scale=1 (int). Functionally equivalent but inspect.signature(fnx.rescale_layout) and inspect.signature(nx.rescale_layout) differ in their default repr, which trips signature-snapshot tests and any tooling that compares default literals (e.g. typer-style CLIs, doc generators). Fix: change default to scale=1 to match nx exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:03:39.139694763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:06:06.809654958Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:06:06.807785158Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 51084e4. rescale_layout default is now scale=1 (int) matching nx exactly. 4 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-wxjtp","title":"parity: utils.rcm + utils.misc test-helpers wrappers (7 functions)","description":"Two clusters of nx utilities not surfaced by franken_networkx. (1) RCM ordering family from networkx.utils.rcm: cuthill_mckee_ordering(G, heuristic), connected_cuthill_mckee_ordering(G, heuristic), pseudo_peripheral_node(G) — companions to the already-shipped reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering. (2) Test/utility helpers from networkx.utils.misc: edges_equal(edges1, edges2, *, directed=False), graphs_equal(g1, g2), nodes_equal(nodes1, nodes2), make_list_of_ints(sequence) — heavily used by nx test suites. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level and adding to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:38:45.270718699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:53:49.014765622Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:53:49.012666712Z","close_reason":"Shipped in d7024ad: RCM ordering family (cuthill_mckee_ordering, connected_cuthill_mckee_ordering, pseudo_peripheral_node) + test helpers (edges_equal, graphs_equal, nodes_equal, make_list_of_ints). All 7 verified on P8/numpy/tuple cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-xd6xi","title":"chordal_graph_cliques rejects MultiGraph but nx accepts and returns cliques","description":"fnx.chordal_graph_cliques raises NetworkXNotImplemented for MultiGraph inputs, but nx accepts them and returns the maximal cliques (treating parallel edges as one).

Repro:
  mg = nx.MultiGraph([(0,1),(1,2),(2,0),(2,3)])
  list(nx.chordal_graph_cliques(mg, backend='networkx'))
  -> [frozenset({0, 1, 2}), frozenset({2, 3})]
  
  fnx.chordal_graph_cliques(fnx.MultiGraph(...))
  -> NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

The comment in fnx claims 'nx enforces via @not_implemented_for' but nx's implementation doesn't actually have that decorator and works on multigraphs. Drop-in code that called chordal_graph_cliques on a MultiGraph hit the spurious exception.

Pre-existing test failures in test_clustering_cliques.py:TestChordalGraphCliquesParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[MultiGraph-...] confirm this defect.

Fix: drop the multigraph rejection. nx's algorithm handles MultiGraph naturally (parallel edges treated as one). Keep the directed-graph rejection since that's a genuine constraint.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:28:27.319773474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:29:21.060932212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-xd6xi","title":"chordal_graph_cliques rejects MultiGraph but nx accepts and returns cliques","description":"fnx.chordal_graph_cliques raises NetworkXNotImplemented for MultiGraph inputs, but nx accepts them and returns the maximal cliques (treating parallel edges as one).

Repro:
  mg = nx.MultiGraph([(0,1),(1,2),(2,0),(2,3)])
  list(nx.chordal_graph_cliques(mg, backend='networkx'))
  -> [frozenset({0, 1, 2}), frozenset({2, 3})]
  
  fnx.chordal_graph_cliques(fnx.MultiGraph(...))
  -> NetworkXNotImplemented: not implemented for multigraph type

The comment in fnx claims 'nx enforces via @not_implemented_for' but nx's implementation doesn't actually have that decorator and works on multigraphs. Drop-in code that called chordal_graph_cliques on a MultiGraph hit the spurious exception.

Pre-existing test failures in test_clustering_cliques.py:TestChordalGraphCliquesParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[MultiGraph-...] confirm this defect.

Fix: drop the multigraph rejection. nx's algorithm handles MultiGraph naturally (parallel edges treated as one). Keep the directed-graph rejection since that's a genuine constraint.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:28:27.319773474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:33:13.979756211Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:33:13.977732181Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xditv","title":"parity: fruchterman_reingold_layout numeric parity with networkx","description":"fnx.fruchterman_reingold_layout / spring_layout share signatures with nx but numeric outputs are not pinned to nx for the same seed and graph. Add a parity test (tests/python/test_layout_parity.py) that builds a small fixed graph (path_graph(6), complete_graph(5), karate_club_graph), seeds with seed=42, and compares fnx vs nx node positions within tolerance 1e-6. If divergence, route to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity when method='auto' to preserve drop-in behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T06:14:51.495203398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:18:38.622702670Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:18:38.620786474Z","close_reason":"Completed in 82fb1eb","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xiqgr","title":"spectral_ordering returns nodes in reversed/different order from nx","description":"fnx.spectral_ordering returns nodes in a different order than nx — mathematically equivalent (the Fiedler eigenvector's sign is arbitrary, so both [a,b,c,d,e] and [e,d,c,b,a] are valid spectral orderings) but the drop-in contract requires matching nx's choice exactly.

Repro:
  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)] (path)
  fnx -> [4, 3, 2, 1, 0]
  nx  -> [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
  same with method='lanczos' produces different tiebreak orderings even on graphs where the default method matches.

Drop-in code that compared spectral_ordering(G) to a reference list (often used as an input to nested-dissection-style ordering or graph layout) silently broke.

Fix: delegate spectral_ordering to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the existing pattern for other delegated algorithms).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:03:30.929863213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:06:32.044081576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:06:32.042029210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xsrkr","title":"parity: Graph instances are content-equal and unhashable, nx uses identity-equal and hashable","description":"fnx.Graph defines content-based __eq__ in the Rust binding (path_graph(3) == path_graph(3) returns True) and is unhashable (Python sets __hash__ = None when __eq__ is defined). nx uses object's default identity-based __eq__ and __hash__: nx.path_graph(3) == nx.path_graph(3) returns False, nx.Graph() is hashable for use in sets/dict keys. Drop-in code that puts graphs in sets/dicts (set([G1, G2])) raises TypeError on fnx; code that compares graph identity (if G is reference_graph or G == reference_graph) gets opposite answers on fnx vs nx. Fix: override __eq__ and __hash__ on all four Graph classes to use object's defaults (identity-based) matching nx exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:42:25.251403517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.218447403Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.216475861Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 64e1627. Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph now use object's identity-based __eq__ and __hash__ matching nx exactly. set/dict containing graphs now works on fnx. 29 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
